content.full_text:
88-464
N/A

FPPC

Dec 12 3:51 PM '09

December 12, 1988


Ms Jeanette Turville
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, 8th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Jeanette,

This letter is a follow-up to our conversation of last week
regarding Proposition 73 and the effects it will have on
municipal campaigns held this spring.

I want to confirm my understanding of our conversation relative
to monies contributed from a controlled committee to my city
council campaign. Specifically, can Good Government in the 80's
(840218) contribute $2,500 to the Tom Bohigian For Fresno
Committee (880129) for the purpose of electing me to the Fresno City
Council? This contribution would be made before January 1, 1988,
and would be spent anytime thereafter, including in 1989. The
election will be held March 7, 1989.

My understanding from talking to you is that this would be
permissable and that after January 1, 1989 I could still use the
$2,500 for such purposes as the purchase of media time, yardsigns,
printing, etc.

I would appreciate confirmation of this at your earliest
convenience. If you could call me at work 209-445-5532 or
home 209-224-6966, it would be helpful. I will check with
you by the end of the week if I have not heard from you. In
advance, I thank you.

Sincerely,

TOM BOHIGIAN
3349 East Holland
Fresno, CA 93726

sections.question: Can Good Government in the 80's (840218) contribute $2,500 to the Tom Bohigian For Fresno committee (880129) for the purpose of electing me to the Fresno City Council? This contribution would be made before January 1, 1989, and would be spent anytime thereafter, including in 1989. After January 1, 1989, could the $2,500 still be used for purposes such as the purchase of media time, yardsigns, printing, etc.?
sections.question_synthetic: The sender seeks confirmation regarding whether a controlled committee can contribute $2,500 to his city council campaign committee before January 1, 1989, with the funds to be spent after that date on campaign expenses, in light of Proposition 73's effects on municipal campaigns.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The sender is seeking advice rather than providing it. No formal CONCLUSION section exists. Document appears to be follow-up to a prior conversation. Date anomaly noted: letter dated December 12, 1988 but references contribution 'before January 1, 1988' which appears to be a typo for 1989.

After January 1, 1989, could the $2,500 still be used for purposes such as the purchase of media time, yardsigns, printing, etc.?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming letter from Tom Bohigian to FPPC staff member Jeanette Turville requesting confirmation of a conversation regarding the permissibility of a $2,500 contribution from a controlled committee to his Fresno City Council campaign, with funds to be contributed before January 1, 1989 but spent thereafter.
